description: A battery manufacturing technician presents for occupational health screening following suspected chronic lead exposure. First, create a lab test type named 'Blood Lead Level Panel' (code: BLL_PANEL) with three analytes and reference ranges: Blood Lead Level (ref: <5 ug/dL), Zinc Protoporphyrin (ref: <40 ug/dL), and Free Erythrocyte Protoporphyrin (ref: <35 ug/dL). Then complete the full intake: Register new patient Marcus Torres (DOB: 1985-06-14, Male, Occupation: Battery Manufacturing Technician). Enter active conditions: T56.0 (Toxic effects of lead and its compounds), D64.9 (Anemia, unspecified), and I10 (Essential hypertension). Document allergy to Penicillin (severe, anaphylaxis). Record family history: father with I25.1 (Atherosclerotic heart disease), deceased at age 62; mother with E11 (Type 2 diabetes mellitus), alive. Create a health evaluation with chief complaint: chronic fatigue, headaches, and abdominal pain for 3 weeks following battery recycling work; vitals: BP 145/92, HR 78, Temp 37.0C. Order the BLL_PANEL you created, plus a CBC and CMP. Prescribe Succimer (DMSA) 10mg/kg every 8 hours for 5 days and Ferrous Sulfate 325mg once daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in 14 days. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
